description_long: This structure is a circular stone temple with two prominent horizontal bands dividing its facade into upper and lower sections. The exterior features evenly spaced columns and large open window-like gaps, giving it a classical and monumental appearance. The roof is flat and ring-shaped, with a slightly raised parapet and no visible dome or spire. The stonework is weathered, with subtle textural variations and a predominantly gray coloration. The interior appears open to the sky, and the structure is elevated on a patterned stone platform. There is minimal ornamentation, emphasizing geometric regularity and symmetry.
description: A circular, columned stone temple with two horizontal bands, open windows, and a flat ring-shaped roof, set on a patterned stone platform. The design is classical and symmetrical, with minimal ornamentation.
description_view_0: Front view shows the circular facade with evenly spaced columns and open windows, highlighting the two horizontal bands and the flat ring-shaped roof.
description_view_1: This side view emphasizes the curvature of the structure and the depth of the open window gaps between columns.
description_view_2: The rear view mirrors the front, showing the same column arrangement and open design, with the patterned platform clearly visible.
description_view_3: Another side view, similar to view 1, but from the opposite angle, again highlighting the circular symmetry and open window spaces.
attribute_ornamentation: minimalist, geometric patterns formed by columns and bands, no visible figurative or intricate carvings
attribute_entrance_design: No distinct main entrance; access is provided by large open window-like gaps between columns, with no visible doors or framing elements.
